headline: The OpenWrt One Router Offers Total Hardware Transparency
lede: For networking enthusiasts, the new OpenWrt One project replaces opaque consumer boxes with fully documentable, user-controlled hardware. It integrates the MediaTek Filogic 820 chipset into a platform designed specifically for firmware customization and deep network visibility.

headline: Navigating Offline with CoMaps
lede: Mapping your journey shouldn't require surrendering your location history to corporate servers. CoMaps provides a stripped-down, privacy-first interface for offline navigation, relying on community-sourced data to guide your path through the woods or across the city.

headline: Open Models Challenge the AI Profit Margin
lede: The rapid rise of interchangeable, high-performance open-weight models is signaling a permanent shift in the artificial intelligence market. As inference becomes commoditized, the traditional economic moats held by proprietary labs face unprecedented pressure.
pullquote: The era of high-margin proprietary AI ecosystems is facing a structural collapse driven by open competition.

headline: How South African Dolosse Changed Harbor Protection
lede: Before the concrete geometric structures known as dolosse, coastal breakwaters were frequently dismantled by the raw power of the ocean. These unassuming, heavy shapes have become a quiet global standard for stabilizing maritime infrastructure against erosion.

headline: Sequencing Personal Genomes from the Home Laboratory
lede: Advances in portable genomic hardware have enabled ambitious hobbyists to move DNA sequencing out of institutional facilities. While the process demands rigor in sample preparation and computational alignment, it offers a tangible look into the biological data of the individual.

headline: Small AI Models Deliver Intelligence to Remote Regions
lede: As pruning and distillation techniques shrink powerful algorithms, sophisticated machine learning is moving from the cloud to the handheld device. This decentralized approach brings vital diagnostic and data-processing tools to communities lacking stable internet connectivity.

headline: Windows Device IDs Enable Persistent User Tracking
lede: Security experts warn that the Global Device ID inherent in Windows installations persists across updates, creating a static identifier for hardware. This mechanism allows for tracking that transcends browser settings, raising fundamental questions about the limits of platform anonymity.

headline: Turning the reMarkable Tablet Into a Magical Diary
lede: By pairing e-ink displays with vision-capable language models, the Riddle project brings a touch of narrative fiction to daily note-taking. This software experiment explores the potential for low-latency, interactive handwriting responses on otherwise static paper-like devices.

headline: Ternlight Runs Embedding Models Inside the Browser
lede: Processing text embeddings entirely within WebAssembly eliminates the need for expensive backend infrastructure and round-trip API calls. Ternlight delivers high-performance semantic search capabilities through a compact, 7 MB client-side package.

headline: Uncovering the Hidden Reasoning Space of Large Language Models
lede: Deep inside the Claude architecture, researchers have mapped internal neural patterns that mirror a human-like workspace for deliberative thought. This Jacobian lens provides a new vantage point to observe how complex machines organize information before final token generation.
